overview: Jeskai Ephemerate is a control deck that leverages Cleansing Wildfire and indestructible artifact lands to ramp and draw cards early. It combines white, blue, and red to provide answers, recursion, and value engines like Archaeomancer. However, its reliance on ramp and answers makes it somewhat slow and vulnerable in the early game.
win_conditions: Archaeomancer combined with Ephemerate for huge value
Token generation from Soul of Migration
Monarch strategy with Crimson Fleet Commodore

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, a turn-1 or turn-2 land, and at least one of: Ephemerate, Lorien Revealed, Counterspell, or a cheap bounce target/creature.
Keep hands that can sequence into a turn-2 Lorien or turn-2 Ephemerate.
Prioritize hands with 2 lands and 3+ spells if you have a turn-1 land.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early interaction or combo pieces, hands with only high-cost spells, or hands lacking a way to protect your combo.

examples.reason: Contains the essential red land, Cleansing Wildfire, and Preordain to ramp and draw into needed answers.

early_game: Prioritize keeping red land and Cleansing Wildfire
Play patiently and avoid aggressive early plays
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap cantrip or hold up mana for interaction.
Turn 2: Cast Lorien Revealed if you have a land, or cast Ephemerate if you have a land and a bounce target.
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit your 2-drop and 3-drop turns.
If opening with Lorien, play land, cast Lorien, and hold up Ephemerate next turn.
If opening with Ephemerate, play land, cast Ephemerate, and hold up Lorien or a creature/land that can be bounced.
mid_game: Control the board by removing key threats
Set up the Archaeomancer and Ephemerate lock
Use removal like Lightning Bolt and Abrade efficiently
Preserve mana for counterspells and bounce effects
never overcommit to the board without protection.
Use the Lorien + Ephemerate loop for card advantage and board wipes, but track opponent's interaction to avoid dead mana.
Manage your hand by keeping answers until you know what the opponent is doing.
Graveyard management is secondary unless facing graveyard-dependent decks.
Commit threats only when you have protection (counterspell, bounce, or a resolved loop).
late_game: Generate massive card advantage with Archaeomancer
Out-resource the opponent to secure the lock
Win with token swarms or monarch
The Lorien Revealed + Ephemerate infinite mana loop is the primary win condition, allowing you to cast multiple spells, board wipes, or finish with Skred.
Skred can also close games quickly if you can bounce it for extra damage or cast it uncounterably.
Use repeated ETB triggers from bounced permanents to overwhelm opponents.
Go all-in when you have protection (counterspell, bounce, or a resolved loop), the opponent is tapped out, or you can close the game before they can interact.
Commit to the loop when you have enough mana to generate card advantage and board control, and when you've tracked that the opponent lacks sweepers or counterspells.

key_cards.name: Cleansing Wildfire
key_cards.notes: Essential for early game mana advantage and card draw.

key_cards.name: Archaeomancer
key_cards.notes: Combined with Ephemerate yields huge value.

key_cards.name: Ephemerate
key_cards.notes: Protects key permanents and synergizes with Archaeomancer.

key_cards.name: Preordain
key_cards.notes: Used to find needed answers during the game.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Key early game answer and part of the removal lock.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Blue interaction to stop opponent's spells.

sequencing_tips: Activate Relic of Progenitus every turn to prevent Gurmag from resolving
Resolve Mulldrifter to stop faeries and gain card advantage
Use Dust to Dust early in Boros matchups to remove artifact lands
Prioritize red land and Cleansing Wildfire in opening hand
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board without protection, misplaying the Lorien + Ephemerate loop, and failing to track opponent's interaction.
Unique interaction: Ephemerate can bounce itself to trigger Lorien repeatedly, generating infinite mana.
Watch for opponent's graveyard hate if they run it.
Format-specific rules may apply, so always check the banned list and format rules.
Practice stack interactions and timing to maximize your combo potential.

matchups.advice: Use Abrade on lands in Game 1, side in Dust to Dust for Game 2 to exile artifacts and indestructible lands.
Play a patience game, resolve Mulldrifter to stop faeries, side in Pyroblast.
Use Archaeomancer, Ephemerate, and Lightning Bolt lock, side in Dawnbringer Cleric and Circle of Protection: Green for hexproof creatures.
Counter and kill the right cards, use Dust to Dust early to remove artifact lands.
Avoid mistakes and bait, focus on generating more resources and locking the opponent.

Sequence spells to trade counterspells, using cheap, uncounterable threats or bounce effects to bypass counterspells. Target permanents already on the battlefield to avoid stack interaction. Use the Lorien loop to generate enough value to out-resource their counterspells.

Apply early pressure and use counterspells to stop key ramp spells. Bounce their mana sources if possible. Close games quickly with Skred before they can stabilize. Use the Lorien loop to draw into answers and out-value their ramp.

Stabilize with counterspells and bounce effects. Use the Lorien loop to draw into answers and stabilize the board. Don't overcommit to the board. Use Skred to pressure their life total while you set up your combo.

Out-value them with card draw and bounce effects. Use Skred to pressure their life total while they are locked down. Force them to use resources on your threats, then close the game with the Lorien loop or repeated ETB triggers.

Win the resource war by using counterspells to protect your combo and disrupt theirs. Bounce their Lorien or Ephemerate to break their loop. Win by out-resourcing, closing faster, or forcing them to make mistakes in the stack.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Damnation, Cruel Ultimatum), counterspells, and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or combo pieces.
Track if they have discard or hand disruption.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: talk about a control deck... improved a lot with Cleansing Wildfire and the indestructible artifact lands... being a little slow, so it suffers a bit on the early game

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Archaeomancer which combined with Ephemerate yields a huge value
creatures like Soul of Migration and the tokens it makes
generate more resources than the opponent and try to lock them

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: activating Relic of Progenitus every turn or even popping it if necessary
resolve our Mulldrifter , which, in addition to stopping the faeries, will give us the card advantage
if well played at the beginning of the game, can be very advantageous
